name: Jetpack
description: A powered traversal state that spends fuel or heat capacity to apply controllable lift and steering while airborne.
design_notes: Jetpack traversal needs a resource boundary and a flight envelope. Define fuel drain, thrust vector, heat or overuse state, fall velocity cap, landing recovery, camera profile, and whether hover is allowed. The mechanic should give short bursts of vertical freedom without invalidating climbing, gliding, elevators, or level boundaries.
design_purpose: Let players gain height, correct falls, or cross gaps using a limited powered movement resource.
Create vertical route planning through fuel, heat, landing spots, and recharge opportunities.
Support science-fiction or mobility fantasies without turning every obstacle into empty airspace.
player_fantasy: The player feels briefly airborne and powerful, managing fuel while steering through vertical space.

parameters.name: jetpack_fuel_capacity_ms
parameters.description: Total powered-flight budget available before recharge or refill.

parameters.name: jetpack_fuel_drain_units_per_second
parameters.description: Fuel ledger drain applied while thrust input is accepted.

parameters.name: thrust_vector_units
parameters.description: Lift and steering vector applied during powered movement.

parameters.name: jetpack_heat_limit_units
parameters.description: Overuse threshold that blocks continued thrust until cooled.

parameters.name: fall_velocity_cap
parameters.description: Maximum downward velocity while thrust assist is active.

parameters.name: landing_recovery_ms
parameters.description: Recovery window after powered landing before full control returns.

edge_cases: Fuel can expire over a lethal pit, requiring a clear fall, glide, or ledge recovery path.
Ceiling collision while thrusting must stop lift without trapping the player in powered state.
Water volumes, low-gravity zones, and wind zones need explicit interaction rules with thrust_vector_units.
Networked jetpacks need server validation for fuel, altitude, and client-predicted thrust.
common_bugs: Thrust is applied in render update and becomes stronger at high frame rates.
Fuel drains after landing because airborne_state_machine does not clear jetpack input ownership.
Heat lockout clears on client but not server, causing repeated correction snaps.
Camera field-of-view changes stack with sprint or glide profiles and create motion discomfort.
balancing_notes: Fuel capacity controls whether jetpack is a correction tool, route tool, or full movement mode.
Hover makes aiming and platforming easier but can trivialize hazards unless fuel drain rises sharply.
Recharge placement determines whether players use jetpack expressively or hoard it for emergencies.
accessibility_notes: Offer reduced camera shake, field-of-view, and vertical bob while powered movement is active.
Allow hold or toggle thrust settings and readable fuel warnings that do not rely only on color.

implementation_notes.unity: Keep jetpack_state with fuel, heat, and thrust vector on the character. Apply lift in FixedUpdate through the same collision owner as other airborne movement, and let the server validate fuel ticks before accepting predicted altitude changes.
implementation_notes.godot: Update jetpack velocity inside _physics_process, sampling fuel_resource before applying upward acceleration. Use Area membership for water or wind modifiers and clear thrust state through landing detection.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use a custom CharacterMovement mode or Gameplay Ability that commits fuel, applies vertical acceleration, and replicates heat_or_overuse_state. CameraManager should read a dedicated jetpack camera profile rather than stacking arbitrary shakes.
implementation_notes.web: Represent fuel, heat, input vector, and airborne state in the fixed simulation loop. Resolve thrust before collision, clamp fall_velocity_cap, and reconcile authoritative fuel when networking is enabled.

relationships.reason: Jetpacks can use fuel-style resource drain and refill transactions to constrain vertical travel.
FOV changes communicate thrust and forward acceleration during powered flight.
Jetpack traversal can bypass oxygen or hazard spaces unless altitude, fuel, and area rules align.

example_games.title: Jetpack Joyride
example_games.note: Uses jetpack thrust as the primary movement verb.

example_games.title: No Man's Sky
example_games.note: Uses jetpack bursts for traversal and fall correction.
